name: Multi-Source Job Search with AI Scoring and Tracking

parameters.text: =Score this job 0-10 for relevance to the user preferences. User wants: {{ $('Normalize Fields').item.json.jobTitle }} in {{ $('Normalize Fields').item.json.location }}, remote: {{ $('Normalize Fields').item.json.remote }}, seniority: {{ $('Normalize Fields').item.json.seniority }}. Job: {{ $json.job_title }} at {{ $json.company_name }}, {{ $json.location }}. Description: {{ $json.job_description }}. Return ONLY: {"score": number, "reason": "one sentence", "fitSummary": "two sentences"}

options.systemMessage: You are a job relevance scoring assistant. Your only output must be a valid JSON object. No explanation, no markdown, no extra text. Only JSON.

parameters.jsCode: const aiOutput = $input.item.json.output || $input.item.json.text || ''; let parsed; try { const jsonMatch = aiOutput.match(/\{[^}]+\}/); parsed = jsonMatch ? JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]) : JSON.parse(aiOutput); } catch (e) { parsed = { score: 0, reason: 'Failed to parse', fitSummary: 'Error in AI response' }; } const jobData = $('Loop Each Job').item.json; return [{ json: { ...jobData, score: parsed.score || 0, reason: parsed.reason || '', fitSummary: parsed.fitSummary || '' } }];

const tenMinutesAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 10 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(); const jobs = $input.item.json.data || []; const filteredJobs = jobs.filter(job => { const jobDate = new Date(job.job_posted_at_datetime_utc); const cutoffDate = new Date(tenMinutesAgo); return jobDate >= cutoffDate; }).map(job => ({ job_id: job.job_id, job_title: job.job_title, company_name: job.employer_name, location: `${job.job_city || ''} ${job.job_country || ''}`.trim(), remote: job.job_is_remote, apply_link: job.job_apply_link, job_description: (job.job_description || '').substring(0, 300), date_posted: job.job_posted_at_datetime_utc, platform_source: job.job_publisher })); return filteredJobs.map(job => ({ json: job }));
